title: TranslateGemma Nama v3 Series - Final Comparison Report

v3.3.description: Baseline: trained on augmented Bible corpus only

data.approach: Single-stage training on Bible translation style

v3.3.notes: Tested on Ruth book comparison; serves as baseline for domain mismatch analysis

v3.4.description: Mixed training: Bible corpus + FLEx dictionary in single stage
v3.4.issue: Domain mismatch: mixing short FLEx entries (~11 words avg) with long Bible sentences (~25 words avg) caused model to specialize for short outputs

stage1_flex.purpose: Build Nama vocabulary and grammar understanding before fine-tuning on Bible

v3.3_to_v3.5_improvement.summary: v3.5 achieves 6x improvement in BLEU over v3.3 baseline

curriculum_learning_benefit.motivation: v3.4 suffered from domain mismatch: mixing short dictionary entries (avg 11 words) with long Bible sentences (avg 25 words) caused the model to specialize for short outputs
curriculum_learning_benefit.solution: 2-stage curriculum: vocabulary pre-training on FLEx, then transfer to Bible translation style
curriculum_learning_benefit.effectiveness: eval_loss improved 47.4% in Stage 2, demonstrating successful knowledge transfer from vocabulary to style alignment

data_efficiency.v3_4_approach: All 25,227 pairs treated equally in single training run
data_efficiency.v3_5_approach: Progressive learning: 2,573 FLEx pairs (Stage 1) → 22,658 Bible pairs (Stage 2)
data_efficiency.benefit: Stage 1 establishes linguistic foundations; Stage 2 refines translation patterns specifically for Bible context

technical_details.adaptation: QLoRA with 32 LoRA rank on 7 transformer modules
technical_details.batch_size: Effective: 32 (2 GPUs × 2 per-device × 8 gradient accumulation steps)

conclusions.key_finding: Curriculum learning is essential for multi-domain translation data
conclusions.recommendation: Deploy v3.5 final_adapter for production use with pastor Park's ministry
next_steps: Human evaluation of sample translations against Nama language standards
Comparison with alternative translation models (if available)
Fine-tuning on additional specialized Bible books (e.g., Gospels, Psalms)

sample_test_statistics.books_represented: Old Testament (Genesis, Exodus, Samuel, Kings, Psalms, Proverbs) and New Testament (Matthew, Mark, Luke, John, Acts, Romans, Corinthians, Galatians, Ephesians, Philippians, Colossians, Thessalonians, Timothy, Titus, James, 1Peter, Hebrews)

notes: This report compares training-phase metrics from v3.3, v3.4, and v3.5. v3.5 demonstrates that 2-stage curriculum learning successfully addresses the domain mismatch problem that hindered v3.4's translation quality.
